title: RNA Extraction with Trizol for cells
author: Maria Cármen Sales

steps_raw.instruction: Remove the culture medium from the plate and add 750μl of Trizol to 1 x 10 5 -10 7 cells. Homogenize with the pipette and transfer to a tube. In this step, you can freeze this sample for up to 6 months in the -80C freezer or continue the protocol. undetermined, Trizol Undetermined, freezer
Incubate for 5 minutes to permit complete dissociation of the nucleoproteins complex. 00:05:00, Incubation
Add 200 μl L of chloroform per 1 mL of Trizol and incubate for 2 to 3 minutes. 00:02:00, Incubation

Centrifuge the sample for 15 minutes at 12,000 xg at 4°C. The mixture separates into a lower red phenol-chloroform, and interphase, and a colorless upper aqueous phase. Undetermined, Centrifugation 00:15:00, Centrifugation
Transfer the aqueous (incolor) phase containing the RNA to a new tube by angling the tube at 45° and pipetting the solution out.
Add 500 μl of isopropanol per 1 mL of Trizol. Incubate for 10 minutes. 00:10:00, Incubation
Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 12,000 × g at 4°C. Undetermined, Centrifugation 00:10:00, Centrifugation
Discard the supernatant with a micropipetto and resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of 75% ethanol per 1 mL of Trizol.
Vortex the sample briefly and centrifuge for 5 minutes at 7500 × g at 4°C. Undetermined, Centrifugation 00:05:00, Centrifugation
Discard the supernatant with a micropipettor. Vacuum or air dry the RNA pellet for 5–10 minutes. 00:05:00, Vacuum or air dry
Resuspend the pellet in 20–50 µL of RNase-free water, 0.1 mM EDTA, or 0.5% SDS solution by pipetting up and down. undetermined, RNase-free water, 0.1 mM EDTA, or 0.5% SDS solution
Incubate in a water bath or heat block set at 55–60°C for 10–15 minutes. Undetermined, water bath 00:10:00, water bath
Store in freezer -80°C until use or make the cDNA reaction then. Undetermined, storage
